title: Under-served-script gap analysis + prioritization shortlist
context: open-accessible-fonts plans to produce fonts for under-served scripts, but no specific script or community partner is secured yet. Before approaching any community, the project needs an evidence-based prioritization shortlist that identifies which scripts have the greatest unmet need for a free, correct, OFL font. This informs outreach and the M2 entry gate.
objective: Produce a gap analysis of under-served writing systems — scripts where free, complete, correctly-shaping OFL font coverage is absent or critically insufficient — and derive a prioritized shortlist (≥5 candidates) with rationale, existing coverage, and suggested community-outreach contacts for each.
acceptanceCriteria: Analysis surveys the major under-served-script candidates (e.g. Adlam, N'Ko, Tifinagh, Vai, Osage, Ol Chiki, Canadian Aboriginal Syllabics, extended-Latin African orthographies) against existing free font coverage (Noto, SIL, community fonts).

Each candidate entry documents: script name, primary language(s) and speaker/learner population, Unicode encoding status, current font coverage gaps (missing weights, broken shaping, missing diacritic forms), and candidate community outreach contacts.
A prioritized shortlist of ≥5 scripts is produced with selection rationale (gap severity, community readiness, Elyos mission fit).
Analysis is honest about data quality: population figures cited with source; font-coverage gaps verified against actual font testing where feasible.
Document is CC-BY-4.0 licensed; no personal data or identifiable contributor information included without consent.
verifiedNeed remains false until a named partner from the shortlist confirms interest.
output: A published gap-analysis document (CC-BY-4.0) covering under-served scripts with a prioritized shortlist of ≥5 candidates, each with coverage gaps documented and outreach contacts suggested, merged into the repository.
